mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, its persistence signals sustained monetary accommodation. This lowers the equity risk premium and discount rates, driving positive equity returns as long as the policy remains in place and inflation expectations are anchored.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's ZIRP policy was implemented in late 2008
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are not statistically different from zero when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% for a sustained period of at least 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening economic recovery, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. This positive fundamental signal drives equity prices higher over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-recession job growth is a primary indicator of recovery
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales signals an unexpected weakening of consumer demand, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This negative surprise leads to downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately 70% of US GDP
failure_condition: In more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a robust supply side of the economy, indicating that firms are successfully meeting demand and expanding operations. This translates to higher expected profitability and supports higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been recovering since mid-2009
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO fails to show a consistently positive rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high (above its natural rate), a modest increase in inflation is interpreted by markets as a sign of returning pricing power and demand, not as a threat that will force premature monetary tightening. This 'good inflation' signal is bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment in 2010 gives the Fed room to tolerate inflation
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.0%, the correlation between 3-month changes in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts signals a recovery in the residential construction sector, a key leading indicator with strong forward linkages to the broader economy. This positive signal boosts investor confidence and drives equity prices higher.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market was the epicenter of the 2008 crisis
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to post positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of the months following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already elevated, a further increase signals a stalled or reversing economic recovery. This raises concerns about future consumer demand and corporate profitability, leading to a sell-off in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 9.7% is a major economic concern
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in months where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: While the Fed holds nominal rates at zero, an acceleration in inflation erodes real interest rates and corporate profit margins. This combination signals that monetary policy is falling behind the curve, increasing the risk of a future sharp tightening cycle and causing a negative re-pricing of equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Market concerns exist about inflation risk under QE policies
regime_hint: zero lower bound (ZLB) with rising inflation
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
